text:
- Utility classes in templates for local layout/state.
- Repeated combinations extracted with `@apply` in `@layer components` (mainly in `static/src/tailwind.css`).
- Page-scoped selectors are intentional (for example `body.landing-page`, `body.legal-page`, `body.help-page`).

Do not "flatten" everything into long utility strings when the page already uses component classes.

---

## 6. Build commands (required after class/token changes)

Run only what is needed:

```bash
npm run build:tailwind
npm run build:tailwind:pipeline
```

Use `watch:*` scripts only for active iterative UI work.

If you add classes in files not currently covered by a config's `content` list, update that config or your classes will not compile into the bundle.

---

## 7. Guardrails and anti-patterns for this codebase

Avoid:
- Tailwind v4 syntax/guidance (`@theme`, configless assumptions).
- Runtime Tailwind CDN on production pipeline pages.
- Introducing new theme mechanisms when page already uses established one.
- Dynamic class name construction that purge/content scanning cannot see.

Prefer:
- Existing token namespaces (`primary`, `secondary`, `neutral`, `dark`, `light`).
- `Poppins` for standard UI typography.
- `JetBrains Mono` only for mono-appropriate content.
- Existing focus/interaction patterns (`focus-visible`, ring/border consistency, accessible states).

Avoid assuming:
- `font-mono` is appropriate for normal UI text
- JetBrains Mono should be used for visual style rather than function
- changing a font token alone is enough without updating imports/base templates

---

## 8. Verification checklist before finishing

1. Rebuild the affected Tailwind bundle(s).
2. For pipeline styling changes, run:
   - `pytest tests/test_pipeline_tailwind_local.py -q`
3. Smoke-check impacted templates for theme parity (light/dark) and no missing classes.
4. Confirm no accidental CDN regression for pipeline stylesheet loading.

---

## 9. Quick decision matrix

Use this when implementing:

- If changing `templates/pipeline.html`/pipeline JS class usage:
  - edit pipeline markup/CSS, use `tailwind.pipeline.config.cjs`, rebuild `build:tailwind:pipeline`.
- If changing landing/legal/help/shared app pages:
  - edit `static/src/tailwind.css` and related templates, use `tailwind.config.cjs`, rebuild `build:tailwind`.
- If changing shared tokens used by both:
  - update both configs (or intentionally keep separate), rebuild both bundles, verify both surfaces.
